mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 1.15% is substantially below the inflation rate implied by CPIAUCSL (246.639), creating negative real rates that incentivize investors to demand higher nominal yields on long-duration assets. A sustained low FEDFUNDS regime signals continued monetary accommodation, which compresses term premiums and reduces 10-year Treasury yields as investors rotate into safer fixed income.
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates negative at current snapshot
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with positive inflation differential
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises more than 15 basis points over the 20-day window despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any rolling 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS at 486553.0 million) remain elevated relative to historical trend, signaling sustained consumer demand. Strong demand pressures force the Fed to maintain or raise rates to combat inflation, which increases the 10-year Treasury yield as market participants price in higher terminal rates and reduced monetary accommodation.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales at elevated nominal levels
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 10 basis points despite RSAFS growth exceeding trigger threshold, or correlation between RSAFS growth and 10Y yield changes becomes negative (r < -0.1) over any 6-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.1% is near historical lows, indicating tight labor market conditions. Tight labor markets generate wage pressure and inflation expectations, forcing the Federal Reserve to signal higher future rates. Market participants reprice long-duration bonds downward, raising 10-year Treasury yields as inflation risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.2% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.15%
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market tightness evident in low unemployment
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage-price spiral risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over the 60-day window despite both UNRATE < 4.2% and PAYEMS growth > 0.15%, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 104.6367) shows moderate growth relative to the 2017=100 baseline. Constrained supply growth combined with elevated demand creates persistent inflation, which the Fed must combat through rate increases. Higher policy rates push 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market prices in a higher neutral rate and extended tightening cycle.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production growth moderate relative to demand signals
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 12 basis points when INDPRO growth falls below 0.2%, or the signal produces negative correlation with yield changes (r < -0.08) over any 9-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST at 1127.0 thousand units) combined with retail sales strength (RSAFS) indicate robust aggregate demand. When both demand proxies are simultaneously elevated, the Fed faces pressure to tighten policy to prevent overheating. Market participants front-run Fed tightening by selling long-duration bonds, raising 10-year Treasury yields.
evidence_refs: fact:Dual demand strength in housing and retail
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across housing and consumption
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines more than 8 basis points despite both HOUST > 1100 and RSAFS growth > 0.3%, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.06 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The spread between inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is approximately 245 basis points, indicating deeply negative real rates. When real rates remain negative for extended periods, investors demand higher nominal yields on long-term bonds to compensate for inflation erosion. This mechanism predicts 10-year Treasury yields will rise as the market reprices inflation risk.
regime_hint: persistent negative real rate regime with inflation above policy rate
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 20 basis points over 60 days despite real rate spread exceeding 200bp, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS at 147010.0 thousand) is at elevated levels. When employment growth accelerates from this base, it signals a tightening labor market that generates wage inflation. Wage inflation forces the Fed to maintain higher rates for longer, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as the market extends its forecast of the tightening cycle duration.
evidence_refs: fact:Employment at elevated levels with tight labor market
regime_hint: strong employment growth in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines more than 5 basis points over the 5-day window despite PAYEMS growth exceeding 0.2%, or the signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 6-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 17156.946 billion chained 2017 dollars) combined with strong retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust aggregate demand growth. When demand growth accelerates above trend, the Fed must raise rates to prevent overheating. Higher policy rates and extended tightening expectations push 10-year Treasury yields upward as investors reprice the duration risk of long-term bonds.
regime_hint: broad-based demand expansion with growth above potential
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls more than 25 basis points over 60 days despite RSAFS QoQ growth > 1.0% and HOUST > 1050, or the multi-proxy signal produces Rank IC < 0.07 over any 18-month rolling window.
